channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: 27 Sheep and Goats Stolen from Limpopo Kraal
item.description: A livestock owner in Makotse, Limpopo, reported the theft of 27 animals (16 sheep and 11 goats) valued at approximately R63,600. The incident occurred between Friday evening and Saturday morning, with...

item.title: Bruce Springsteen Criticizes Trump, Sparks Outcry from MAGA Supporters
item.description: In a PBS special titled 'Bruce Springsteen: Finding America in Song,' the musician criticized President Donald Trump, calling him a 'coward' and advocating for 'critical patriotism.' Springsteen empha...

item.title: California's Ballot Measures: Key Propositions and Their Implications
item.description: California's upcoming ballot measures have sparked intense political and public debate, with multiple proposals addressing critical issues such as housing, healthcare, wealth taxation, and environment...

item.title: PCSO Major Lotto Games Have No Jackpot Winners on July 6, 2026
item.description: The Philippine Charity Sweepstakes Office (PCSO) announced that no one won the jackpot prizes in its major lotto draws on July 6, 2026. The Grand Lotto 6/55 jackpot, valued at P63,065,055.92, remains ...

item.title: Trump urges FIFA to review US player's red card suspension amid Belgium's challenge
item.description: President Donald Trump has admitted calling FIFA president Gianni Infantino to request a review of a red card issued to US forward Folarin Balogun during the World Cup. The incident occurred after Bal...

item.title: Study warns of potential risks of zero-sugar diets in mice
item.description: A recent study on mice reveals that a strict zero-sugar diet may have hidden dangers, challenging the popular belief that eliminating sugar entirely is always beneficial. While mice on such a diet did...

item.title: Residents Push for Better Accessibility in Regina Neighbourhoods
item.description: A local group in Regina, Saskatchewan, is advocating for improved accessibility across the city, with particular focus on the Demarco and Second Avenue area. Barrier Free Saskatchewan, a community-dri...

item.title: University of Ibadan Law Student Released After Abduction
item.description: A 300-level Law student from the University of Ibadan, Miss Fumilayo, has been safely released after being abducted alongside others. The Students' Union Government (SUG) confirmed her freedom in a st...

item.title: Scientists Complete Mapping of Earth's Submerged Eighth Continent, Zealandia
item.description: Zealandia, a submerged landmass surrounding New Zealand and New Caledonia, has been fully mapped after decades of geological research. This achievement completes the documentation of its nearly two mi...

item.title: Tanzania hosts Africa's largest SACCO congress to boost financial inclusion
item.description: Tanzania is set to host the largest gathering of savings and credit cooperative leaders on the African continent, marking a significant milestone for the country's cooperative movement. The 26th Savin...

item.title: FIFA Rejects Belgium's Appeal Against Folarin Balogun's Suspension, Allowing USA Star to Play in World Cup Match
item.description: The FIFA Appeal Committee has rejected Belgium's appeal against the suspension of USA star Folarin Balogun, allowing him to participate in the World Cup round of 16 match against Belgium. The decision...

item.title: Arthur Boyd's Nebuchadnezzar Series Explored at Bundanon Art Museum
item.description: The Bundanon Art Museum has unveiled a major exhibition featuring Arthur Boyd's anti-Vietnam War Nebuchadnezzar series, showcasing over 50 artworks spanning decades. The exhibition, titled 'Man on Fir...

item.title: Lahore Court Acquits Man in Blasphemy Case Due to Lack of Evidence
item.description: A Lahore sessions court acquitted a man accused of desecrating the Holy Quran, ruling that the prosecution failed to establish a direct link between the accused and the alleged blasphemy act. The case...

item.title: Alberta and Ontario Propose New West-East Oil Pipeline and Minerals Corridor
item.description: Alberta Premier Danielle Smith and Ontario Premier Doug Ford have announced a new proposal for the Northern Shield Energy Corridor, a 3,300-kilometre pipeline transporting Alberta oil to southern Onta...

item.title: Tanzania's Boxing Team Prepares for Commonwealth Games with Medal Ambitions
item.description: The national boxing team of Tanzania, under the guidance of head coach Samweli Kapungu, is in the final stages of preparation for the upcoming Commonwealth Games in Glasgow, Scotland. The event, sched...

item.title: Raigad Police and Volunteers Rescue Eight Bus Passengers, Including 72-Year-Old Woman, from Floodwaters
item.description: A rescue operation in Maharashtra's Raigad district ensured the safe evacuation of eight passengers after a state-run ST bus became stranded in floodwaters caused by continuous heavy rainfall. The ope...

item.title: Two men granted bail after police roadblock incident involving blue lights
item.description: Two men, Afzal Abramjee and Manuel Pinto, were granted bail of R10,000 each by the Pretoria magistrate’s court following a police roadblock incident. The case originated from a stop in Wierdabrug on J...

item.title: Paolini's Victory Boosts Confidence in Tennis Match Against Eala
item.description: Jasmine Paolini, the Italian tennis player, showcased renewed confidence after securing a crucial victory against her opponent, Eala, in a recent match. The win marks a significant milestone in her ca...

item.title: Kenya and Gates Foundation Launch 10-Year Plan to Expand Irrigation
item.description: The Kenyan government, in collaboration with the Gates Foundation, has launched an ambitious 10-year plan to expand irrigation by one million acres. This initiative aims to boost food security, create...

item.title: Australians Shift Toward EVs and Hybrids as Sales Reach Half Market Share
item.description: The article highlights a significant shift in Australia's vehicle market, with electric vehicles (EVs) and hybrids now accounting for half of all new car sales. This marks a turning point after years ...
